blurb:
A Good Samaritan spotted Caspian and made sure he got somewhere safe. He landed at Mostly Mutts, found a great foster home, and has been making himself very comfortable ever since. He's 8 months old, about 9.5 pounds, and still growing into himself.

His foster describes him as sweet and affectionate, and the specifics back that up. He wants to be near you. Lap sitting is his preferred activity, but he's equally happy playing when you're in the mood for it. He gets excited about treats. He keeps his litter box clean without being asked. He checks a lot of boxes without making a big deal about it.

He gets along well with the other cats in his foster home, so a multi-cat household could work well for him. How he does with dogs is unknown since he hasn't had the chance to meet one yet. He's been good with kids and does best when they're gentle and know how to handle a cat. Bring your crew in for a meet and greet before committing.

Mostly Mutts is a rescue based in Kennesaw. Visit their website to submit an inquiry about Caspian and schedule a visit.
shelter_hours: See website for current hours and adoption appointment availability
scoring_notes:
• Lap-sitting, treat-loving, litter-box-reliable young cat with almost no adoption friction — broadest audience appeal
• Good Samaritan rescue origin adds a feel-good hook without requiring a heavy backstory
• Appears recently placed in foster with no urgency signals — lower priority for limited newsletter space

blurb:
Judy is a one-year-old tuxedo cat who's been living her best life in foster care, charming everyone she meets, and waiting patiently while her siblings all got adopted before her. She's got a sleek coat, a big purr, and a heart that literally has a tiny hole in it. That last part sounds more dramatic than it is. The condition is called a ventricular septal defect, and she's had two echocardiograms that show she's doing well. You'd never know anything was different about her watching her tear around with a feather wand.

She's playful and curious but not chaotic. She likes a lively home, just not a circus. When she's done running the show, she'll find a soft blanket and curl up. She also plays independently, so you don't have to entertain her every second.

Judy has met dogs, cats, and kids and handled all of it with confidence, though she does best when introductions are slow and she gets to come around on her own terms. She gave a dog the nose-to-nose sniff and decided he was fine. She face-rubbed a couple of six and seven-year-olds into submission. She gets along great with the two adult cats in her foster home. She just needs someone willing to let her do things at her pace.

Mostly Mutts is a rescue org based in the Atlanta area. Head to their website to learn more about Judy and submit an inquiry to get the process started.
shelter_address: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue – Kennesaw, GA (foster-based rescue; no public facility)
scoring_notes:
• Heart condition is well-monitored and manageable, but may give adopters pause — worth addressing head-on in the newsletter
• Emotionally resonant underdog angle: last of her litter still waiting while siblings all found homes
• Strong compatibility profile (kids, cats, dogs) makes her a broadly appealing candidate despite the medical footnote
